title: 新手想學寫網站，好多野唔明....

replies.msg:
最近發覺讀緊嗰科好乞食，想學返門世藝搵食，所以就上網學下啲Web Develop嘅嘢。
搵完一排發覺太多野，眼都花，所以上嚟想問下啲唔明嘅嘢：

1. 以我理解大部分情況下一個四舊嘢嘅stack，DB+Backend Framework+Frontend framework+Backend Tech已經足夠應付好多嘢，咁咩時候要用到Docker/AWS Lambda之類嘅tech?

2. 我自己學緊嘅stack係Vuejs+Firebase，咁樣會有啲咩限制?值唔值得去花時間學返啲實際嘅backend嘢例如node express mongodb咁?

3. 一般嘅full stack/frontend developer需唔需要學啲devops or workflow嘅相關知識？我連testing都冇做過...

4. 我自己最弱嗰項係css，如果淨係識用framework例如material/bulma/bootstrap，公司會唔會請？咩scss/postcss/less咁q多種睇到頭都暈

5. 通常大家係點樣搵到第一份工作？又係幾時(學到咩地步，識寫啲乜出嚟)開始搵到？pt又好ft又好freelance都好。如果想搵啲最entry level可以邊做邊學嘅，有冇辦法？

6. 對新嘅programming初學者例如我有冇咩建議？

唔該哂大家

寫網頁一步步黎啦
level 1 At least 識用 HTML, CSS, JS, Jquery 寫d 簡單 static wesite

level 2 開始學下d frontend framework (angular 2, React, Vue, etc) 同時接觸下backend scripting language (PHP, nodejs, ruby,GO, Python(Django,flask), Java). 因為學 framework同時要接觸埋ajax, routing呢d 涉及backend 既概念。(個人偏好React & nodejs)

level 3 Backend API. 砌 restful api可以玩埋DB, Security (TLS, OAuth, etc.) 既東西

見到樓上有人話JSP流行真係得啖笑,
香港多人用係主流冇錯,但主流唔同流行,
你剩係想有份CODING工做下的話學冇問題,
但如果你係想係WEB哩方面深入鑽研的話,
都係學返啲新野好啲,nodejs, php, ruby之類都幾好,
不思進取唔想學新野不如唔好學CODING啦

Tech trend 變得快
想跟得上唔係見新野學新野，而係打好個底
呢d野我覺得係基本：

基本data structure and algo
唔同既programming paradigm (OO, 近年復興既functional, etc)
Http / https (client-server model, for web dev)
Web service (SOAP and REST)
Character encoding
binary arithmetic (對理解一個programming lang既data type有用)
clean code / refactoring (readability同簡潔係極緊要，上面有人話寫comment重要，其實好既readable code係越少comment越好，用well named variable/class/method黎reflect作者intention先係好，好既program係好似一篇好既prose)
Git (version control)
Basic OS knowledge (可以學埋Linux command, for server admin)
DB concept (relational, NoSQL)

我自己係非CS degree出身，好多野都係做緊野果陣自己學（雖然好多野都唔識，仲學緊），明白基礎既重要

上面有咩唔齊歡迎補充

Google's software engineers develop the next-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect, explore, and interact with information and one another. Our products need to handle information at massive scale, and extend well beyond web search. We're looking for engineers who bring fresh ideas from all areas, including information retrieval, distributed computing, large-scale system design, networking and data storage, security, artificial intelligence, natural language processing, UI design and mobile; the list goes on and is growing every day. As a software engineer, you will work on a specific project critical to Google’s needs with opportunities to switch teams and projects as you and our fast-paced business grow and evolve. We need our engineers to be versatile, display leadership qualities and be enthusiastic to take on new problems across the full-stack as we continue to push technology forward.
Do you want to help Google build next-generation web applications like Inbox, Gmail, Google Search, Google Maps and more? As a Front End Software Engineer at Google, you will specialize in building responsive and elegant web applications that scale to millions of users in dozens of languages.
Google is and always will be an engineering company. We hire people with a broad set of technical skills who are ready to take on some of technology's greatest challenges and make an impact on millions, if not billions, of users. At Google, engineers not only revolutionize search, they routinely work on massive scalability and storage solutions, large-scale applications and entirely new platforms for developers around the world. From AdWords to Chrome, Android to YouTube, Social to Local, Google engineers are changing the world one technological achievement after another.
Responsibilities
Design, develop, test, deploy, maintain and improve software.
Manage individual project priorities, deadlines and deliverables.
Qualifications

Minimum qualifications:
BA/BS degree in Computer Science or related technical field or equivalent practical experience.
1 year of work experience.
Experience in JavaScript, and one or more programming languages including but not limited to: Java, C/C++, Python or Go.
Experience with front end technologies and/or front end frameworks.

Preferred qualifications:
Experience developing user-facing software.
Experience with the latest and greatest web standards, including HTML5 and CSS3.
Knowledge of web libraries and frameworks such as AngularJS, Polymer, Closure or Backbone.
Strong sense of web design and attuned to the fundamentals of user experience.
Familiarity with the whole web stack, including protocols and web server optimization techniques.
An understanding of the principles of accessibility and can build products that are accessible to users with disabilities.

唔好意思，整亂左你個post
1. Docker係用黎搞VM既野黎，eg 你要一部電腦同時開window+linux 就要用佢la，我好少搞infrastructure
2. vuejs 聽聞不錯的，工都有唔少，如好多巴打講，Firebase 好少人會咁用，因為好多野要customzie同埋Google 未必上到大陸
3. 要識，至少要識testing，因為好多時testing 可以幫你del 到好多bug，同埋如果寫test寫得好，有D人勁到睇test = 睇 doc
btw 想知咩係testing 可以睇下D 咩TDD先
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Test-driven_development
4. framework 要識用之餘都要識pure css，因為好多library 未必比到足夠既flexible你去改個layout，呢個時候好多時要靠CSS去夾硬改。
至於scss/postcss/less多數係因為css 無computation 既功能, eg 開variable，所以就有呢D野去GEN CSS令佢變得可以programmable
學唔學就要睇公司用開邊隻la
5. 我係靠profressor 彈比我先既 你或者睇下有無朋友要做相關既野lo
如果去D freelance 網要小心傾requirement。唔係做到你hihi
6.
最重要係找清楚你要解決既問題先可以寫到code
我見過有人係window 試行Log system
主要係寫D野入個file
但成日無lala 輕左
淨係睇住堆code De 左成日bug 又比佢老闆係咁小

香港開始有nodejs工既時候
外國d blog已經一早吹奏緊go

如果樓主真係有心學，我建議學兩隻language 起手

JavaScript 雖然可以Frontend backend 通殺 (而且node已經好成熟, MEAN stack 香港搵工唔難，可以玩熟佢)
但係初學者黎講，如果分唔清兩邊關係，撈到亂哂咁寫會更加亂
所以最好都係Frontend 寫react/vue, backend 揀個Python + django (或者java都得，php就唔好喇 ) 理解下基本back end 概念，砌個簡單API 俾自己個Frontend call 去行得郁成隻web app, 咁個learning會完整d同埋難d撈亂, 之後再學nodejs都唔遲

至於devops 果d去到日常工作就必要，但等到你可以望住d tutorial 可以行得郁自己個local 再學啦，唔係會浪費時間係setup environment度

最後係搵工，作為startup人當然會建議你搵startup學野，但的確易中伏 (比較似係你本身識行內既人知道有咩startup有勁人就可以minimize中伏機會咁)
退而求其次 可以考慮cherrypicks 果d agency, 佢地tech唔會太弱，只係唔係做自己既產品咁解，對於白紙level黎講唔會無野學

但實際上香港就算係startup 都有d有香港style既弱智野，例如 deadline 大於一切然後set勁rigid deadline既文化，所以唔敢包

一路都唔明點解會有人將Lang 分難易度 通常我dup code之前 會將d細節寫出黎，畫出黎，一定要清楚自己應該去fulfill d咩requirement 心裡面有哂做法，點樣做，考慮哂唔同情況，例如係咪會用到好重型既Multi-thread , 會唔會deploy 去唔同server 到，定係只係一d簡單task, 好似係定期向某d server 問佢拎d file 咁
